title: Soy wax jar candle with citrus and spice
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with sweet orange essential oil and spice fragrance oil
excerpt: A soy wax container candle with sweet orange essential oil and a spiced-fruit fragrance oil, suited to autumn and winter. Burns approximately 30-35 hours.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry candle formulation guides (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): blending EO and fragrance oil in soy wax. CandleScience fragrance load guidance for soy container wax (candlescience.com, accessed May 2026).

content.text: A soy wax container candle with a two-part scent: sweet orange essential oil for the bright top note and a spiced-fruit fragrance oil for the warm base. Together they give the kind of scent profile associated with autumn and winter kitchens, without being obviously festive. Blending an essential oil with a fragrance oil is a practical way to keep the top note fresh while extending the staying power of the overall fragrance.
The sweet orange EO has a lower flash point than the fragrance oil. Add both at the lower end of the
range (55°C) to avoid volatilising the citrus top notes before the wax sets. Melt the wax over a simmering-water bath. Never leave melting wax unattended; keep a fire blanket within reach.

items.name: Sweet orange essential oil
items.prepNote: 3.5% of wax weight. Cold-pressed sweet orange peel oil. Add at 55°C.

items.name: Candle fragrance oil
items.prepNote: 2.5% of wax weight. A spiced-fruit or warm spice candle-grade fragrance oil (clove, cinnamon, apple, or a combination). Add alongside the sweet orange EO at 55°C.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: CD-12 or ECO-8 for a 7 cm diameter jar.

content.text: Set the wick and melt the wax
Fix the wick tab to the jar base, thread through the centring bar. Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher over a simmering-water bath. Allow to cool to 55°C before adding the fragrance.

Mix the sweet orange essential oil and the spice fragrance oil together first, then add the combined blend to the wax at 55°C. Stir gently for 2 minutes. Pour into the prepared jar slowly, filling to 1 cm below the rim. Allow to set undisturbed at room temperature for 24 hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. The
should reach the jar edge before extinguishing; allow 3-4 hours. Trim the wick before each subsequent burn.

Keep in a cool, dark place away from sunlight. The soy wax is indefinite unlit; the sweet orange top notes fade after 18 months. The spice base notes hold longer. Use within 2 years for the best combined scent.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (~30-35 burn hours)
